system.description: It grows in fresh water, lengthening its long winding tendrils almost to the surface. When touched, it snatches and pulls, coiling down to the bottom and holding for a few minutes before relaxing back up.
system.additional_info:
Is their growth boosted supernaturally, and can you see faces reflecting from the Ghost Field beneath their fronds? Are the locals aware of the threat, willing to talk about it?

Dangers:
• Snatchweed is cultivated in areas where intruders must enter the water to get past other obstacles.
• The bottom of the water has two foot spikes, victims are pulled down onto them.

Remedies:
• A sign warns of the hazard, as required by law.
• This particular breed of snatchweed recoils from salt; put enough on the surface and tendrils recoil.
